TestCase z23Change(TestCase t1) {
    TestCase follow_case = t1;
    // --- Memory handling code generated based on the rule ---
    if (t1.n1 > 0 && t1.a != NULL) {
        double* follow_case_a = (double*)malloc(t1.n1 * sizeof(double));
        if (follow_case_a != NULL) {
            memcpy(follow_case_a, t1.a, t1.n1 * sizeof(double));
            follow_case.a = follow_case_a;
        } else {
            // malloc failed, return the original struct to avoid NULL pointer usage
            return follow_case;
        }
    } else {
        // No allocation needed if n1 <= 0 or null pointer
        follow_case.a = NULL;
    }
    // ------------------------------------

    // Defensive code generated based on rule (a) to prevent errors when n1 <= 0
    if (follow_case.n1 <= 0 || follow_case.a == NULL) {
        // No elements or null array, return immediately
        return follow_case;
    }

    // Specific operation code generated based on the rule
    int half = follow_case.n1 / 2;
    for (int i = 0; i < half; i++) {
        follow_case.a[i] += 10.0;
    }

    return follow_case;
}
